title: cerastium arvense f. glandulosum
intro: Cerastium arvense f. glandulosum, also known as sticky mouse-ear chickweed, is a flowering plant in the pink family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense f. glandulosum include its potential to soothe and calm skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and its ability to promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Cerastium arvense f. glandulosum is often used as a topical application, either as a salve, ointment, or infusion, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
